ddls:
CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "substation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the substation where the measurement was taken */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that recorded the measurement */,
  "hour" INTEGER /* Hour of the measurement */,
  "t_sec" INTEGER /* Time in seconds within the hour */,
  "v1" REAL /* Voltage V1 (Phase 1) */,
  "vangle1" REAL /* Phase angle of V1 (Phase 1) */,
  "v2" REAL /* Voltage V2 (Phase 2) */,
  "vangle2" REAL /* Phase angle of V2 (Phase 2) */,
  "v3" REAL /* Voltage V3 (Phase 3) */,
  "vangle3" REAL /* Phase angle of V3 (Phase 3) */,
  "i1" REAL /* Current I1 (Phase 1) */,
  "iangle1" REAL /* Phase angle of I1 (Phase 1) */,
  "i2" REAL /* Current I2 (Phase 2) */,
  "iangle2" REAL /* Phase angle of I2 (Phase 2) */,
  "i3" REAL /* Current I3 (Phase 3) */,
  "iangle3" REAL /* Phase angle of I3 (Phase 3) */,
  "frequency" REAL /* Frequency of the electrical system */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature at the measurement point */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity at the measurement point */,
  "power_factor" REAL /* Power factor */,
  "interval_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the measurement interval */,
  "measurement_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the measurement type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_interval_id FOREIGN KEY ("interval_id") REFERENCES measurement_intervals ("interval_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_measurement_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_type_id") REFERENCES measurement_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_substation_id FOREIGN KEY ("substation_id") REFERENCES substations ("substation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "substations" (
  "substation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each substation */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the substation */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the substation */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the substation */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the substation */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the substation */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the substation */,
  "commission_date" TEXT /* Date when the substation was commissioned */,
  "decommission_date" TEXT /* Date when the substation was decommissioned (if applicable) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the substation (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("substation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "substation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the substation where the sensor is installed */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor (e.g., Voltage, Current) */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the sensor (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the sensor */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the sensor for identification */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was last calibrated */,
  "calibration_due" TEXT /* Date when the next calibration is due */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_substation_id FOREIGN KEY ("substation_id") REFERENCES substations ("substation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurement_intervals" (
  "interval_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interval */,
  "interval_name" TEXT /* Name of the interval (e.g., Every Second, Every Minute) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the interval */,
  "interval_length" INTEGER /* Length of the interval in seconds */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Whether the interval is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interval_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurement_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the measurement type (e.g., Voltage, Current) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the measurement type */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement */,
  "range_min" REAL /* Minimum value range for the measurement type */,
  "range_max" REAL /* Maximum value range for the measurement type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, operator, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user for quick contact */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the measurement */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the measurement was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., Voltage Drop, Phase Imbalance) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message of the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was generated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., Unresolved, Resolved) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Actions taken in response to the alert */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "generation_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., Daily, Weekly, Monthly) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_maintenance" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance activity */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor being maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the maintenance activity */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance activity (e.g., Completed, Pending) */,
  "performed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_maintenance_performed_by FOREIGN KEY ("performed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_maintenance_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "substation_maintenance" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance activity */,
  "substation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the substation being maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the maintenance activity */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance activity (e.g., Completed, Pending) */,
  "performed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_substation_maintenance_substation_id FOREIGN KEY ("substation_id") REFERENCES substations ("substation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_substation_maintenance_performed_by FOREIGN KEY ("performed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
